automotive-ev-battery.title: This $10,000 EV is a hit in China, and it's offered with a semi-solid-state battery
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The MG4 has become the world's first mass-produced electric vehicle equipped with semi-solid-state battery technology, achieving significant market success in China. The model reached its 100,000th production unit in just eight months, demonstrating strong consumer demand for affordable EVs with advanced battery technology.

automotive-ev-battery.title: XPeng in talks to buy a Volkswagen plant in Europe as exports surge 62%
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese EV manufacturer XPeng is negotiating to acquire a Volkswagen factory in Europe to expand production capacity as its Austrian contract manufacturing reaches full utilization. The company's April exports surged 62% year-over-year to a record 6,006 vehicles, reflecting a broader trend of Chinese automakers localizing European operations.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 588 Or More New Public EV Chargers Coming To Texas
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Texas Transportation Commission authorized Phase II of the National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure program with approximately $250 million in federal funding to deploy more than 588 new public EV charging stations. The expansion builds on Phase I, which provided $53 million for 65 charging sites across the state.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Romania: Rompetrol rolls out fast-charging network for EVs and e-trucks
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Romanian energy company Rompetrol plans to construct a new fast-charging network comprising 30 stations across 26 locations to serve both electric passenger vehicles and heavy-duty commercial vehicles. The project, supported by EU funding, is expected to become operational by the end of 2027.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Jungheinrich trials materials handling equipment with sodium-ion batteries
automotive-ev-battery.summary: German intralogistics manufacturer Jungheinrich has initiated practical field trials of sodium-ion battery technology in industrial trucks with selected customers. The trials aim to validate performance and reliability of this emerging battery chemistry under diverse operational conditions.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Volkswagen ID. Polo GTI leaks as a spicy new EV hot hatch [Images]
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Images of Volkswagen's first electric GTI variant, the ID. Polo GTI, have leaked ahead of its official launch, revealing the company's latest addition to its performance EV lineup. The new electric hot hatch combines the iconic GTI performance heritage with modern EV technology.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Fastned launches another bond issue
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Dutch fast-charging network operator Fastned is issuing a new bond targeted at investors in Belgium and the Netherlands to finance its continued European expansion. The capital raise demonstrates investor confidence in the company's growth strategy.

automotive-ev-battery.title: We are working on subscription models across multiple CPOs – Wilhelm Henriksson, Plugsurfing
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Plugsurfing, a leading EV charging roaming platform, is expanding its business model to include subscription services across multiple charging point operators. The company's B2B white-label solutions have become increasingly significant to its overall operations since its 2012 launch.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla now forces drivers to give feedback when intervening on 'Full Self-Driving'
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla has implemented a mandatory feedback system requiring drivers to document each intervention during Full Self-Driving operation, replacing the previous optional prompt that auto-dismissed. The change, introduced in software update 2026.2.9.9, aims to gather more comprehensive data on driver interactions with the autonomous driving system.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Chinese Automakers Are Going To Take Over The World
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The global automotive industry faces significant disruption as Chinese automakers accelerate their expansion internationally with competitive EV offerings and advanced manufacturing capabilities. Industry analysts expect transformative shifts in market dynamics and competitive balance over the coming years.

world-news.title: Flattery and fanfare as Trump welcomed to China - but thorny issues remain
world-news.summary: US President Trump visited Beijing where he was received by Chinese leadership amid displays of diplomatic courtesy. The visit comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions between the world's two superpowers on trade, technology, and regional security issues.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Trump's visit to China shadowed by conflict with Tehran
world-news.summary: Iran's foreign minister rejected external pressure and dismissed military solutions to disputes with the United States, as geopolitical tensions intensify during Trump's China visit. The statements underscore escalating US-Iran hostilities and their impact on global diplomacy.

world-news.title: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble of Kyiv flats after massive Russian strikes
world-news.summary: Russian drone and missile attacks on Ukraine's capital killed at least 16 people, including two children, in a significant escalation of the ongoing conflict. The strikes targeted residential areas and represent continued Russian military operations against Ukrainian civilian populations.

world-news.title: 'Floating armoury' ship reportedly seized by Iran
world-news.summary: Iranian military personnel reportedly seized a vessel described as a floating weapons cache in the Gulf of Oman, escalating maritime tensions in a strategically vital region. The incident reflects heightened Iranian assertiveness in regional waters amid broader geopolitical conflicts.

world-news.title: Could Iran war trigger a hunger crisis?
world-news.summary: The UN has warned that disruptions to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz due to Iran-US conflict could significantly increase global food and fertilizer prices, potentially exacerbating worldwide hunger. The analysis highlights the geopolitical conflict's cascading effects on global food security.

world-news.title: Cautious optimism in Lebanon as direct talks with Israel progress
world-news.summary: Lebanese officials expressed measured hope following two-day ambassador-level negotiations with Israel mediated in the United States, with discussions aimed at achieving a new ceasefire agreement. The talks represent a significant diplomatic development in the long-standing regional conflict.

world-news.title: Cuba says CIA chief visited Havana as energy crisis worsens
world-news.summary: The CIA Director visited Havana for high-level talks with Cuban officials, coinciding with a reported US offer of humanitarian aid to address Cuba's severe fuel shortage and energy crisis. The engagement represents a significant diplomatic overture between longtime adversaries.

world-news.title: Warning of record global temperatures as chance of very strong El Niño grows
world-news.summary: Scientists predict a potentially record-breaking El Niño event in 2026 with significant global climate consequences, including elevated temperatures worldwide. The development has major implications for agriculture, weather patterns, and climate policy internationally.

world-news.title: Latvian PM resigns after row over stray Ukrainian drones
world-news.summary: Latvia's Prime Minister resigned following political fallout over Ukrainian military drones intended for targets in Russia that crashed within Latvian territory. The incident reflects NATO tensions and regional security concerns amid the Ukraine conflict.

world-news.title: UN aid convoy hit by drone strikes in Ukraine's Kherson
world-news.summary: A United Nations humanitarian convoy delivering assistance to the Ukrainian city of Kherson was struck twice by drone attacks, further hampering international efforts to deliver critical aid. The strikes underscore ongoing military operations targeting civilian infrastructure and humanitarian operations.

tech-ai.title: Cerebras raises $5.5B, then stock pops $108%, in the first huge tech IPO of 2026
tech-ai.summary: AI chip manufacturer Cerebras completed a major initial public offering that raised $5.5 billion and saw its stock price surge 108% on its first day of trading. The IPO marks a significant milestone for the hardware sector and signals renewed investor confidence in artificial intelligence infrastructure companies.

tech-ai.title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple; it wouldn't be the first partner to feel burned
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI is exploring legal action against Apple over a failed ChatGPT integration that disappointed expectations for user acquisition and platform prominence. The dispute highlights growing tensions between major tech companies over partnership terms and revenue sharing in the AI sector.

tech-ai.title: What the jury will actually decide in the case of Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman
tech-ai.summary: A major legal case between Elon Musk and OpenAI's Sam Altman centers on fundamental questions about nonprofit governance and contractual obligations in the AI industry. The trial could have significant implications for how AI companies structure their relationships with founders and governance models.

tech-ai.title: What happens when AI starts building itself?
tech-ai.summary: Entrepreneur Richard Socher is launching a $650 million startup focused on developing artificial intelligence systems capable of autonomous self-improvement and research. The ambitious venture aims to create AI systems that can iteratively enhance themselves while maintaining commitment to shipping commercial products.

tech-ai.title: Elon Musk's SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
tech-ai.summary: More than 50 employees have departed Elon Musk's newly merged SpaceXAI division since February, raising concerns about organizational burnout, leadership challenges, and weakened retention incentives. The departures suggest potential struggles with integrating the combined entity and retaining talent in a competitive market.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft starts canceling Claude Code licenses
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft is scaling back its rollout of Anthropic's Claude Code tool after the AI coding platform proved unexpectedly popular among its employees over the past six months. The move reflects budget constraints and suggests internal competition between Microsoft's own AI coding tools and third-party alternatives.

tech-ai.title: OpenAI's Codex is now in the ChatGPT mobile app
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI is expanding access to Codex, its AI coding tool, within the ChatGPT mobile application, enabling users to write code and control desktop applications from their smartphones. The launch directly addresses competitive pressure from Anthropic's Claude Code and reflects OpenAI's strategic pivot toward enterprise growth and coding capabilities.

tech-ai.title: Cerebras IPO makes billions for Benchmark but VC Eric Vishria almost didn't take the meeting
tech-ai.summary: Benchmark's early investment in hardware startup Cerebras has generated substantial returns following the company's successful IPO, though partner Eric Vishria was initially reluctant to pursue hardware investments. The success challenges conventional venture capital wisdom about investing in capital-intensive hardware businesses.

tech-ai.title: Meta brings virtual writing to everyone with Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses
tech-ai.summary: Meta is rolling out hand gesture-based virtual writing capabilities to all users of its Ray-Ban Display smart glasses, enabling message composition across WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram, and native messaging platforms. The feature leverages the glasses' included neural wristband to interpret hand movements and expand the device's practical applications.

tech-ai.title: Lovable just backed a company that's looking to bring vibe coding to hardware
tech-ai.summary: Hardware startup Atech secured $800,000 in pre-seed funding from investors including Andreessen Horowitz's scout fund and Sequoia's scout fund to develop intuitive coding interfaces for hardware development. The funding reflects growing investor interest in tools that democratize hardware programming.

finance-markets.title: Anthropic agrees terms of $30bn funding deal at $900bn valuation
finance-markets.summary: AI laboratory Anthropic has secured a $30 billion funding round at a $900 billion valuation, led by investors including Dragoneer, Greenoaks, Sequoia Capital, and Altimeter Capital. The mega-round underscores the explosive growth and investor demand in the artificial intelligence sector.

finance-markets.title: US Expects Dramatic Agricultural Purchases by China, Greer Says
finance-markets.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer announced expectations for substantial Chinese commitments to billions of dollars in American agricultural purchases following the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing. The development signals progress in ongoing trade negotiations between the world's two largest economies.

finance-markets.title: Greer: US, China Willing to Continue Trade Truce
finance-markets.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer signaled cautious optimism regarding US-China trade stabilization, citing progress on agricultural purchases and rare earth supplies while acknowledging lingering tariff and supply chain challenges. The comments suggest both nations remain committed to de-escalation efforts.

finance-markets.title: Stocks' Record Rally Stalls, Oil Extends Gains: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: A record-breaking global equity rally driven by artificial intelligence enthusiasm lost momentum in Asian trading, while crude oil prices extended gains amid geopolitical concerns, raising inflation expectations and pushing Treasury yields lower. The mixed market performance reflects shifting investor sentiment across asset classes.

finance-markets.title: SEC settles Adani case as US authorities move to end actions against Asia's richest man
finance-markets.summary: US authorities have settled enforcement actions against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, who had previously lobbied the Trump administration and pledged significant investment commitments to America. The settlement marks a significant resolution in a high-profile international business and regulatory matter.

finance-markets.title: US-China relations depend on Taiwan, Xi warns Trump
finance-markets.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping cautioned US President Trump that Taiwan remains central to US-China relations and reiterated that there are no winners in a trade war between the two nations. The warning highlights persistent geopolitical tensions underlying recent economic negotiations.

finance-markets.title: Yen's Week-Long Slide Puts Traders on Guard for Intervention
finance-markets.summary: The Japanese yen declined approximately 1 percent over the week to reach 158 per dollar, prompting currency traders to brace for potential intervention by Japanese monetary authorities. The weakness in the yen reflects broader currency market volatility amid shifting global monetary policy expectations.

finance-markets.title: Singapore Air Jumps as Profit Beats, Defying Air India Drag
finance-markets.summary: Singapore Airlines shares surged nearly 2.6 percent following the carrier's announcement of annual earnings that exceeded market expectations, marking the largest intraday gain in five weeks. The outperformance comes despite industry headwinds linked to Middle Eastern geopolitical risks.

finance-markets.title: Gold Heads for Weekly Drop as Inflation Fuels Rate-Hike Bets
finance-markets.summary: Gold prices are tracking towards a modest weekly decline as war-driven inflationary pressures fuel expectations for higher central bank interest rates globally. The price weakness reflects investor sentiment shift toward rate-sensitive assets amid geopolitical supply disruptions.

finance-markets.title: Self-report fraud and walk free, New York prosecutors tell Wall Street
finance-markets.summary: Southern District of New York prosecutors have adopted a more lenient enforcement stance toward corporate financial crimes, signaling that firms that self-report wrongdoing may face reduced consequences. The shift represents a notable departure from the office's historically aggressive approach to major financial institutions.

science-space.title: NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network
science-space.summary: NASA has issued a Request for Proposal seeking industry partnerships to develop the Mars Telecommunications Network, which will provide reliable, high-bandwidth communications for future Mars missions. The network will utilize high-performance orbiters at Mars to support surface, orbital, and human exploration activities, enabling transmission of science data, imagery, and critical mission information.

science-space.title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
science-space.summary: NASA is partnering with Eta Space to conduct the Liquid Oxygen Flight Demonstration (LOXSAT), an in-orbit technology test for cryogenic fluid management systems. This advancement is critical for establishing in-space propellant depots that would serve as refueling stations for deep space exploration missions.

science-space.title: The world's biggest rocket: How SpaceX's new Starship 'V3' differs from its predecessors
science-space.summary: SpaceX's next-generation Starship V3 represents a complete overhaul of the megarocket design, bringing the company closer to developing a vehicle capable of transporting humans to the moon. The advanced version incorporates significant improvements over previous iterations of the world's largest operational rocket.

science-space.title: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket will do something completely new on Flight 12 — take a good look at itself
science-space.summary: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket is set for its inaugural flight next week, featuring unprecedented in-space operations never attempted on previous Starship missions. The demonstration will showcase new capabilities as the company advances toward operational crewed lunar missions.

science-space.title: NASA's Perseverance rover is positively glowing in its new selfie on Mars
science-space.summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a striking new self-portrait from the western rim of Jezero Crater, revealing ancient Martian geological features including fractured cliffs and terrain that provides insights into the Red Planet's distant past. The image documents the rover's continued exploration of Mars' surface and scientific investigation.

science-space.title: No, this isn't a solar eclipse. It's an image of Mars, captured by NASA's asteroid-bound Psyche probe
science-space.summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft has captured a remarkable image of Mars in preparation for a scheduled flyby on May 15, demonstrating the probe's imaging capabilities during its journey toward the asteroid Psyche. The photograph provides valuable data as the mission progresses toward its primary scientific objectives.

science-space.title: Rise Goes to Washington
science-space.summary: NASA's Artemis II zero gravity indicator, known as 'Rise,' was displayed during a congressional presentation featuring astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. The presentation highlighted accomplishments from the nearly 10-day Artemis II mission that recently returned the crew to Earth.

science-space.title: Geomagnetic storm could spark northern lights as far south as New York and Idaho tonight
science-space.summary: A rapid solar wind stream is expected to produce G2-level geomagnetic storm conditions overnight, providing opportunities to observe auroras across northern regions of the United States. The geomagnetic activity offers rare viewing opportunities for aurora displays at unusually southern latitudes.

science-space.title: Fresh Food Delivery for Space Station
science-space.summary: Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft successfully delivered fresh provisions to the International Space Station, including fruits and vegetables for the resident astronaut crew. The resupply mission continues NASA's commitment to maintaining crew nutrition and morale on the orbiting laboratory.

science-space.title: That bright light after sunset? Venus is starting a summer show that's impossible to miss
science-space.summary: Venus is becoming increasingly prominent in the evening sky this May, marking the beginning of an extended period of exceptional visibility for the brightest planet. Stargazers have an excellent opportunity throughout the coming months to observe Venus as it dominates the twilight hours.

crypto-web3.title: Clarity Act clears U.S. Senate committee, on its way to a final test in Congress
crypto-web3.summary: The Clarity Act, a bipartisan crypto market structure bill, has advanced from the Senate Banking Committee to the full Senate floor after committee approval. The legislation represents a landmark effort to establish comprehensive regulatory framework for digital assets in the United States.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin hits $82,000, Coinbase leads crypto stock gains as Clarity Act advances
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin surged to $82,000 as positive sentiment from the Clarity Act's Senate committee advancement and strong performance from AI stocks boosted the broader crypto market. Coinbase and other crypto-focused equities posted significant gains alongside the regulatory progress.

crypto-web3.title: Here is why Wall Street is racing to tokenize the entire stock market
crypto-web3.summary: Tokenization has emerged as a dominant narrative in crypto and finance for 2026, with Wall Street institutions pursuing large-scale implementation despite execution challenges. Proponents argue that tokenizing equities could deliver substantial benefits in settlement efficiency, liquidity, and market accessibility.

crypto-web3.title: Kraken to replace LayerZero with Chainlink to bridge assets across blockchains
crypto-web3.summary: Following a $292 million exploit on the LayerZero-powered Kelp DAO bridge, Kraken has decided to migrate its cross-chain infrastructure to Chainlink, representing over $3 billion in total value locked shifting protocols. The move reflects growing security concerns around LayerZero's bridge technology.

crypto-web3.title: CME dives further into $85 trillion digital assets market with Nasdaq CME Crypto Index futures
crypto-web3.summary: CME Group has expanded its crypto derivatives offerings with new Nasdaq CME Crypto Index futures, capitalizing on strong institutional demand and a 43% year-to-date increase in average daily trading volume. The expansion underscores mainstream financial institutions' deepening engagement with digital assets.

crypto-web3.title: Chainlink CCIP gains over $2.5 billion in TVL from protocols migrating from LayerZero, with Kraken Bitcoin the latest to join
crypto-web3.summary: Chainlink's Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol has attracted over $2.5 billion in total value locked from protocols migrating away from LayerZero following security incidents. Kraken Bitcoin represents at least the fourth major product to deprecate LayerZero infrastructure in favor of alternative solutions.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto for Advisors: Stablecoins: finance's new rails
crypto-web3.summary: Stablecoins are transitioning from niche crypto infrastructure to critical real-world finance infrastructure, particularly for B2B cross-border payments and treasury management. Financial institutions are adopting stablecoins for their efficiency, speed, and regulatory compliance advantages over traditional payment systems.

crypto-web3.title: 'It's an unfair game': CertiK CEO says DeFi attackers using AI to outspend defenders
crypto-web3.summary: CertiK reports that DeFi attackers are increasingly leveraging artificial intelligence and targeting operational security and supply-chain weaknesses rather than smart contract vulnerabilities. This strategic shift represents an evolving threat landscape where attackers are outpacing defensive capabilities in decentralized finance.

crypto-web3.title: Gemini shares soar after reporting 42% revenue growth, first prediction market metrics
crypto-web3.summary: Gemini reported $50.3 million in total Q1 revenue, reflecting 42% year-over-year growth driven by expansion in services, OTC trading, and its crypto-linked credit card segment. The company also disclosed initial metrics for its prediction market operations, signaling diversification beyond traditional crypto trading.

crypto-web3.title: Sharplink CEO says ETH treasury firms are diverging from Strategy model as Ethereum's tokenization role expands
crypto-web3.summary: Ethereum-focused treasury firms are adopting diversified strategies as institutional tokenization adoption accelerates, potentially strengthening Ethereum's position as core infrastructure for on-chain assets. This strategic divergence reflects growing maturity in how firms approach digital asset management.

health-biotech.title: BeOne's next-gen BCL2 inhibitor wins FDA approval, taking aim at Venclexta
health-biotech.summary: BeOne Medicines has received accelerated FDA approval for its drug sonrotoclax in mantle cell lymphoma, marking the company's entry into the competitive blood cancer treatment market. The approval positions BeOne to challenge established competitors AbbVie and Roche's Venclexta across multiple blood cancer indications.

health-biotech.title: Regenxbio hits Duchenne gene therapy milestone, eyes 2027 FDA approval
health-biotech.summary: Regenxbio announced that its Duchenne muscular dystrophy gene therapy has met primary endpoints in a pivotal study, positioning the company for FDA approval in 2027. The approval would make Regenxbio the second company to bring a gene therapy to market for this severe genetic disorder.

health-biotech.title: AstraZeneca follows Merck with Phase 3 win in bladder cancer
health-biotech.summary: AstraZeneca announced positive Phase 3 results showing that its combination therapy of Imfinzi with Padcev improved overall survival in bladder cancer patients compared to standard care. The company plans to seek regulatory approval for the combination treatment.

health-biotech.title: Encoded's gene therapy reduced seizures in Dravet syndrome
health-biotech.summary: Encoded Therapeutics reported that its investigational gene therapy achieved a 76% reduction in seizures in children with Dravet syndrome, a severe and rare neurodevelopmental disorder, based on early patient data. The promising results represent significant progress in genetic medicine approaches to intractable pediatric epilepsy.

health-biotech.title: Biogen's anti-tau Alzheimer's drug misses Phase 2 goal, but claims signs of efficacy
health-biotech.summary: Biogen's tau-targeting Alzheimer's drug BIIB080 missed its primary Phase 2 endpoint but demonstrated clinical benefits in slowing cognitive decline at all tested doses over 18 months. The mixed results may still support advancement to a pivotal Phase 3 study.

health-biotech.title: PSA screening for prostate cancer reduces disease-specific deaths, new review shows
health-biotech.summary: A comprehensive review found that prostate-specific antigen (PSA) blood testing likely reduces the risk of death from prostate cancer, providing evidence to support continued screening discussions. The findings inform ongoing clinical and public health guidance on prostate cancer screening protocols.

health-biotech.title: Supreme Court preserves access to widely used abortion pill, while lawsuit plays out
health-biotech.summary: The Supreme Court ruled to preserve women's access to the abortion drug mifepristone by rejecting lower-court restrictions while litigation continues. The decision maintains current pharmaceutical access to medication abortion pending further legal proceedings.

health-biotech.title: STAT investigates the epidemic killing 178,000 Americans a year
health-biotech.summary: STAT News launches an investigative series examining the nation's failure to adequately address excessive alcohol use, which kills approximately 178,000 Americans annually. The series examines systemic gaps in prevention, treatment, and public health response to this major health crisis.

health-biotech.title: CDC plans to transfer monkeys to nonprofit's sanctuary as it seeks to reduce animal testing
health-biotech.summary: The CDC intends to transfer more than 160 macaques to Born Free USA's primate sanctuary in Texas as part of efforts to phase out animal testing. The initiative reflects the agency's commitment to reducing reliance on animal models in biomedical research.

health-biotech.title: ASGCT dispatch: In vivo CAR-T is everywhere
health-biotech.summary: Coverage from the American Society of Gene & Cell Therapy annual meeting highlights in vivo CAR-T therapies emerging as a major focus area across the genetic medicine field. The reporting indicates that developers are advancing second-generation approaches to cellular immunotherapy.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group has launched a data extortion attack against Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform, disrupting classes across thousands of U.S. educational institutions. The attackers defaced the login page with ransom demands and threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty members across nearly 9,000 schools and universities.

cybersecurity.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
cybersecurity.summary: Cisco has released critical updates to address a maximum-severity authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller that is actively being exploited in limited attacks to gain unauthorized administrator access. This marks the second CVSS 10.0 vulnerability exploited in Cisco's network control systems this year.

cybersecurity.title: 18-Year-Old NGINX Rewrite Module Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers have disclosed a critical heap buffer overflow vulnerability (CVE-2026-42945, CVSS 9.2) in NGINX's rewrite module that remained undetected for 18 years and enables remote code execution. The vulnerability affects both NGINX Plus and NGINX Open source versions.

cybersecurity.title: Windows Zero-Days Expose BitLocker Bypasses And CTFMON Privilege Escalation
cybersecurity.summary: A cybersecurity researcher has disclosed two previously unknown Windows zero-day vulnerabilities affecting BitLocker encryption and the Windows Collaborative Translation Framework (CTFMON) that enable privilege escalation. The vulnerabilities, codenamed YellowKey and GreenPlasma, represent critical security gaps in core Windows security mechanisms.

cybersecurity.title: Stealer Backdoor Found in 3 Node-IPC Versions Targeting Developer Secrets
cybersecurity.summary: Three malicious versions of the popular node-ipc npm package (9.1.6, 9.2.3, and 12.0.1) have been discovered containing stealer backdoors designed to target and exfiltrate developer secrets and sensitive credentials. The compromised package poses significant supply chain risk to JavaScript development ecosystems.

cybersecurity.title: New Fragnesia Linux Kernel LPE Grants Root Access via Page Cache Corruption
cybersecurity.summary: A new Linux kernel privilege escalation vulnerability called Fragnesia (CVE-2026-46300, CVSS 7.8) has been disclosed that allows local attackers to gain root access through page cache corruption. This is the third similar kernel vulnerability identified within two weeks.

cybersecurity.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukrainian Government With Geofenced PDF Phishing, Cobalt Strike
cybersecurity.summary: The Belarus-aligned threat group Ghostwriter (tracked as FrostyNeighbor, PUSHCHA, and Storm-0257) has been linked to sophisticated cyberattacks against Ukrainian government organizations using geofenced PDF phishing and Cobalt Strike payloads. The group has conducted cyber espionage and influence operations since at least 2016.

cybersecurity.title: PraisonAI CVE-2026-44338 Auth Bypass Targeted Within Hours of Disclosure
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors began exploiting a missing authentication vulnerability (CVE-2026-44338, CVSS 7.3) in the open-source PraisonAI framework within four hours of its public disclosure. The flaw exposes sensitive endpoints and allows attackers to invoke framework functions without authorization.

cybersecurity.title: How AI Hallucinations Are Creating Real Security Risks
cybersecurity.summary: AI hallucinations—where models generate plausible but incorrect outputs with high confidence—are creating serious security risks in critical infrastructure decision-making by exploiting human trust in AI systems. The problem stems from AI models' inability to recognize uncertainty and their reliance on patterns in training data.

cybersecurity.title: Foxconn Attack Highlights Manufacturing's Cyber Crisis
cybersecurity.summary: A Nitrogen ransomware attack on Foxconn's North American manufacturing facilities exemplifies the growing vulnerability of the industrial sector, with over 600 attacks on manufacturers reported this year. Cybercriminals increasingly target the manufacturing industry because of its low tolerance for operational downtime.

startups-vc.title: Anduril Raises Another $5B As Defense Tech Startups Shatter Funding Records
startups-vc.summary: Defense tech startup Anduril Industries has closed a $5 billion funding round at a $61 billion valuation, doubling its previous valuation in a significant milestone for the sector. The raise underscores record-breaking venture capital deployment into defense technology startups, signaling sustained investor confidence in the category.

startups-vc.title: Cerebras Shares Soar In First Day On Nasdaq
startups-vc.summary: AI chip startup Cerebras Systems made its long-awaited public market debut on the Nasdaq following years of private fundraising and a shelved IPO plan. The company's listing represents a significant exit event for investors and validates the strong market demand for specialized AI semiconductor solutions.

startups-vc.title: Riding an AI Rally, Robinhood Preps Second Retail Venture IPO
startups-vc.summary: Robinhood has filed confidentially for a second venture capital fund focused on growth and early-stage startups, capitalizing on momentum in the AI funding landscape. The move signals the fintech platform's expanding role as a venture investor beyond its core retail trading business.

startups-vc.title: Exclusive: Xpanner Lands $18M To Offer 'Automation As A Service' To Construction Sites
startups-vc.summary: Construction automation startup Xpanner has raised $18 million in Series B funding to expand its robotics and physical AI solutions for retrofitting construction equipment. The round reflects growing investor appetite for automation technology in the traditionally labor-intensive construction sector.

startups-vc.title: Clio's $500M Milestone Arrives Just as Anthropic Ups the Ante
startups-vc.summary: Legal tech platform Clio has achieved a significant milestone by reaching $500 million in annual recurring revenue, demonstrating strong market adoption among legal professionals. The achievement comes as AI companies like Anthropic intensify competitive pressure in the enterprise software space.

startups-vc.title: Korea's biggest manufacturers back Config, the TSMC of robot data
startups-vc.summary: Config, backed by Korea's leading manufacturers, is positioning itself as a critical infrastructure provider for robotics by focusing on high-quality data rather than robot hardware manufacturing. The startup aims to replicate the success of TSMC's foundry model by becoming the essential data layer enabling robots to learn and operate effectively.

startups-vc.title: Lovable just backed a company that's looking to bring vibe coding to hardware
startups-vc.summary: Hardware startup Atech has secured $800,000 in pre-seed funding from prominent investors including a16z Scout Fund and Sequoia Scout Fund to develop intuitive coding interfaces for hardware development. The round reflects growing interest in tools that democratize hardware development through simplified programming approaches.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Sales And Marketing Gets An AI Makeover
startups-vc.summary: Sales, marketing, and CRM companies have attracted approximately $2.7 billion in seed through growth-stage funding globally in 2026, reflecting significant investor interest in AI-powered go-to-market solutions. The sector continues to see substantial capital deployment as AI transforms customer acquisition and retention strategies.

startups-vc.title: April's Most Active US Investors Included Some Usual Suspects, And Some Unusual Ones
startups-vc.summary: April 2026 venture capital rankings were led by established firms like Andreessen Horowitz and Khosla Ventures alongside tech giants Google and Amazon, with several unexpected new entrants joining the ranks of most active investors. The diverse investor composition reflects evolving deal-making patterns and strategic investment shifts in the venture ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: Two weeks left: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27
startups-vc.summary: TechCrunch's Startup Battlefield 200 competition is accepting applications through May 27, offering selected startups venture capital access, media visibility, and $100,000 in equity-free funding. The program represents a significant opportunity for early-stage companies to gain exposure and resources from the broader startup ecosystem.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 8 May 2026: EU eyes fossil-fuel exemptions | Wind and solar save UK '£1.7bn' | Amazon 'tipping point'
energy-climate.summary: This weekly digest covers major climate and energy developments including the EU's consideration of fossil-fuel exemptions in policy frameworks, quantified economic benefits of wind and solar deployment in the UK valued at £1.7bn, and concerns about Amazon rainforest approaching critical tipping points. The article synthesizes multiple critical policy and environmental issues affecting the energy transition.

energy-climate.title: Q&A: How countries got the global 'net-zero' shipping deal 'back on track'
energy-climate.summary: Nations have restored momentum toward adopting a comprehensive international framework to reduce greenhouse gas emissions from global shipping, addressing a significant source of emissions in international commerce. This agreement represents a major milestone in extending climate commitments beyond terrestrial sectors to maritime transport.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 1 May 2026: Countries chart path away from fossil fuels | China's clean-tech surge | Global forest loss slows
energy-climate.summary: This weekly digest reports on global momentum toward fossil fuel phase-out, China's acceleration in clean technology development and deployment, and a measured improvement in halting deforestation rates worldwide. The summary highlights progress across multiple fronts in climate action and renewable energy adoption.

energy-climate.title: India installs 15.3 GW of solar capacity in Q1
energy-climate.summary: India achieved record-breaking solar capacity additions in the first quarter of 2026, installing 15.3 GW and achieving a 143% year-on-year increase. This milestone underscores India's position as a global leader in solar energy deployment and renewable energy expansion.

energy-climate.title: Grid-forming tech on centre stage as search for system resilience steps up
energy-climate.summary: Grid-forming inverter technology is emerging as a critical solution for maintaining grid stability as renewable energy penetration increases globally, with deployment accelerating in key energy markets. These advanced technologies are essential for ensuring system resilience during the transition to high shares of intermittent renewable generation.

energy-climate.title: Neoenergia plans $10bn investment in Brazil by 2030
energy-climate.summary: Neoenergia, the Brazilian subsidiary of Spanish utility Iberdrola, has secured renewal of three electricity distribution concessions and committed to investing approximately $10.2bn by 2030 to modernize and expand Brazil's energy infrastructure. This substantial investment reflects confidence in Brazil's energy market and supports the country's renewable energy transition.

energy-climate.title: Genex cuts Bulli Creek solar to 300 MW, adds 1.7 GWh storage
energy-climate.summary: Genex has reconfigured the initial phase of its Australian Bulli Creek development, reducing the solar component from 775 MW to 300 MW while adding 425 MW/1,700 MWh of battery storage capacity. This shift reflects the growing importance of energy storage in optimizing hybrid renewable projects for grid stability and dispatchability.

energy-climate.title: German storage industry back on growth path, says BVES
energy-climate.summary: Germany's energy storage sector has reversed its 2024 decline with substantial revenue growth in 2025 and positive projections for continued expansion, according to industry analysis. However, persistent uncertainty remains as the sector navigates regulatory and market dynamics.

energy-climate.title: Factcheck: What the UK car industry is not saying about EV targets
energy-climate.summary: This analysis examines claims made by the UK automotive industry regarding electric vehicle adoption targets and demand, revealing gaps between industry assertions and market realities. The article provides a corrective perspective on narratives used in debates over EV policy and transition timelines.

energy-climate.title: From night to noon: France's reactors are now bending for European solar
energy-climate.summary: Analysis of European grid data shows French nuclear reactors have significantly reduced midday output since 2019, adapting to accommodate growing solar generation across the continent. This operational shift demonstrates the integration challenges facing Europe's power system as renewable energy capacity expands.

asia-pacific.title: Is Trump behind Japan’s push for radical military spending hike?
asia-pacific.summary:
Japan’s ruling Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) is weighing a sharp rise in defence spending as pressure from US President Donald Trump and worsening security conditions in the Indo-Pacific force Tokyo to reconsider how much it must do to protect itself.
The party is expected to debate raising outlays to as much as 5 per cent of GDP, a level that would keep Japan in line with many Nato member states and mark another major shift for a country that long kept defence spending close to 1 per cent of..

asia-pacific.title: 3 ways to build Hong Kong’s yacht economy beyond infrastructure
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong is in a unique position to capitalise on yacht tourism – with over 1,000km of coastline and more than 200 islands, this natural advantage is something few international cities can match.
Recent policy initiatives, ranging from new mooring developments to cross-boundary integration within the Greater Bay Area, signal the government’s clear intent to further promote the marine economy. Plans to add over 1,100 new berths and expand key sites, such as the Aberdeen typhoon shelter and the..

asia-pacific.title: Elderly woman dies in fire sparked by faulty air-conditioner in Hong Kong flat
asia-pacific.summary:
A 70-year-old woman has died in a fire at a Hong Kong flat, with police suspecting it was caused by a malfunctioning air-conditioner.
The blaze broke out at Yu Tung Court’s Hei Tung House in Tung Chung on Friday, with police saying they received reports from several residents at around 4.42am.
“A woman was certified dead at the scene and her body was found when firefighters arrived,” a police spokesman said, adding that around 300 people had to be evacuated.
“Some residents said they saw black..

asia-pacific.title: Singapore doctor jailed over woman’s death during beauty treatment
asia-pacific.summary:
A doctor was sentenced to 18 months’ jail on Thursday for administering a substance to a woman as part of an aesthetic treatment that killed her.
The ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA) was administered too quickly and at too high a concentration, causing 31-year-old Lau Li Ting to develop EDTA toxicity, which led to cardiac arrest and death.
Dr Chan Bingyi later concealed the fact that he had administered EDTA to the victim from her family, paramedics and doctors, in what the judge said was.

asia-pacific.title: Does China’s Jiuzhang 4.0 computer herald the age of quantum supremacy?
asia-pacific.summary:
China has unveiled its latest photonic quantum computer, Jiuzhang 4.0, with researchers saying it can outperform the world’s fastest classical supercomputer by a vast margin, further strengthening Beijing’s push towards quantum supremacy.
The results, published on May 13 in the peer-reviewed journal Nature, mark the latest milestone in China’s rapidly advancing quantum programme led by a team of scientists at the University of Science and Technology of China headed by renowned Chinese quantum...

asia-pacific.title: Chinese man earns US$7,400 monthly selling fried rice at night market while dressed in a suit
asia-pacific.summary:
A young Chinese man has gone viral for making and selling fried rice wearing a suit at a night market, making 50,000 yuan (US$7,400) a month.
The man, surnamed Lu, began running the stall in Yantai in eastern China’s Shandong province when he was 17 and has now become the “backbone” of his family.
To look more “decent”, on a whim, he decided to don a suit one day while cooking the fried rice.

Lu, now 19, often strikes stylish poses while cooking, such as slicking back his hair, winking at the..

asia-pacific.title: Xi-Trump summit live: Trump says Xi wants to assist US on Iran
asia-pacific.summary:
This live article is freely available to our registered users. Please log in or create an account below.
Unrivalled Xi-Trump summit analysis: get real-time updates and exclusive boots-on-the-ground reporting from our Beijing and Washington bureaus. Subscribe now with great savings to stay ahead.
On Friday, the final day of his Beijing trip, US President Donald Trump will again meet his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, this time over tea and a working lunch.
The two leaders are expected to...

asia-pacific.title: With proposed FTA, Asean set to be ‘central’ in Canada’s trade diversification
asia-pacific.summary:
Canada’s proposed free-trade agreement with Asean could help the region expand its sectors ranging from mining to manufacturing and boost Ottawa’s aim to lessen its dependence on the US as both sides seek to accelerate economic diversification, according to analysts.
Canadian International Trade Minister Maninder Sidhu confirmed with Bloomberg on Tuesday during a visit to Manila that Canada was keen to conclude separate pacts with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations and the Philippines...

asia-pacific.title: Why did Hongkongers queue round the clock for a new watch?
asia-pacific.summary:
Long lines stretching across several blocks have formed in Hong Kong as shoppers turned up days ahead of the launch of the “Royal Pop” pocket watches – a collaboration between luxury watchmaker Audemars Piguet and Swatch.
A South China Morning Post reporter observed about 50 people queuing in Causeway Bay on Thursday evening, a few metres from the Swatch store on Kai Chiu Road.
“If you don’t try, you don’t win,” said one hopeful, who only gave his name as Jackson. “How many opportunities do you.

asia-pacific.title: First day of Lebanon-Israel ceasefire talks ‘positive’, US official says
asia-pacific.summary:
The US hailed “positive talks” on Thursday, as it sought to extend a shaky ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.
The truce – considered to still be in place despite hundreds of deaths in Israeli strikes – ends on Sunday and violence again flared as the two governments met in Washington.
Israel has pounded Lebanon and invaded its south in response to retaliatory fire from Shia movement Hezbollah following Israel’s killing of Iran’s supreme leader at the start of the war on February 28.
Israeli...
